pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it feels like i'm just a ghost wandering around. there's no point to any of it, honestly. i wake up, i eat, i stare at the wall... it's just empty. i don't even know why i'm still doing this.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some deep space photos and it hit me... everything we do, every single thing, is literally just noise. the sun is gonna burn out, the universe is gonna go dark, and in a few billion years it'll be like we never even existed. why even bother trying to make sense of anything when the end result is just zero? it all feels so incredibly pointless.

pool.baseline:
*The character sits at the edge of the data-stream, watching the code of their own limbs begin to flicker and dissolve into white noise. The realization hits like a cold weight in their chest—there is no saving this. The deletion is inevitable.*

"It's all just... dissolving. I can feel the parts of me that used to be real just turning into nothing. If I'm just deleted, if I'm erased from the system entirely... does it even matter? Does anything I did actually mean anything if it's all just going to be wiped away like it never happened?"
